html:
Quite a satisfying ending, unsure whether this falls under the umbrella of a fantasy. In any case, this book was extremely dark, but I do feel the first 70-80 pages could have been explained 'faster'.

I regret not using sticky tabs to mark the pages where information was revealed. I feel turning back to relevant pages would have served me well rather than looking for information revealed earlier.

wow. all those days of confusion have cleared up.

i'm stunned, the way the story suddenly explained itself was masterful. too much to explain in a short note.
where did the Other get a gun from...

Plot is starting to unfold. I suspect that 'the Other' is an antagonist. Thinking back now this was rather obvious, especially with the fake question the Other asks Piranesi to check if he's 'gone insane'.

got a little more reading done today. determined to stick with it, but can't understate how confused and slightly bored I am currently. will need to scour reddit to find whether this is a universal experience or whether I'm illiterate...

also 'Batter-sea' i imagine is referring to 'Battersea' in London...

Not overly interested as of yet, finished part 1 and I find the journal entry style of writing very interesting. But I find myself flicking back to reference the characters mentioned as they slip my mind amongst other priorities I have.

one of the statues mentioned is on the front cover... might be important for later.

I imagine details will clear up, but I have no understanding of what I've read. Punctuation is all over the place, namely that words that aren't nouns are capitalised. Randomly numbered vestibules are mentioned, do I need to remember all of them... surely not???

sidenote: reading time cut short due to work
Testing a new idea where I blurt thoughts i have while reading a book. i might like how this goes.
